Mayor Daggett this morning is
sued a call on the board of public
works for an investigation Of all
the railroad crossings in Spokane.
His action follows the wrecking of
a street car on Howard street.


Commissioner ONO declared yes
terday that the Northern Pacific
had promised to fix this crossing.
Today, after making an investiga-
ton, the board of public works re-
ports that all the railroad crossings
in the city age unsafe.


The mayor made the following
request of the board:


Gentlemen: I beg to request
your honorable body to make a
thorough and complete investiga-
ton of the conditions existing at
the various railroad crossing with
in the business districts of the city,
with a view to seeing whether the
proper precautions are being taken
by the railway companies 10 pre-
vent accidents to the public using
such crossings.


note by the newspaper accounts
of the serious accident yesterday
on Howard street that the gates
there were out Of repair and not
working, and beg to direct your
special attention to the character
of the precautions that are being
taken by the railway companies for
the care Of the public, and to see
whether, in your opinion, they are
suffi cient.


l will be pleased to have any
recommendations that your board
may desire to make after your in
vestigation.

quest that the board of public
